data.title: Celanese shares rise on adjustments to operations amid supply-chain concerns
data.description:
Celanese Corporation (CE) outlined a series of operational changes aimed at maintaining supply continuity as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East reshape customer priorities.

The company said security of supply has become a renewed focus for customers, prompting adjustments across its global production network. Celanese (CE) shares rose 3.5% on Monday morning following the announcement.

The company said it restarted its vinyl acetate monomer unit in Frankfurt, Germany, on an accelerated schedule. It also began commissioning a new vinyl acetate ethylene emulsions reactor at the same site, which is expected to add capacity and lower costs.

The company is working to complete maintenance on its polyoxymethylene unit in Frankfurt, with a restart targeted for April.

U.S. supply redirected to support Europe

To support the restart, Celanese (CE) said it is redirecting methanol produced at its Fairway Methanol joint venture in Clear Lake, Texas, toward its German operations. The material will be used to supply the polyoxymethylene unit once it resumes operations in the second quarter.

The company said its integrated production chain allows it to shift inputs such as methanol and acetic acid into higher-value downstream products depending on demand.

Executives highlight flexibility

Mark Murray, senior vice president of the acetyls business, in a statement said the company’s existing operating model allows it to respond to disruptions in supply and demand.

Todd Elliott, senior vice president of engineered materials, said the company is continuing to adjust its global network in response to changing market conditions.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Monday: REPL, GS, LEG and more
data.description:
Stock futures cautious on Monday as investors reacted to a breakdown in peace talks between the U.S. and Iran over the weekend. Tensions spiked after President Donald Trump threatened a military blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a move that quickly pushed oil prices back above the $100-a-barrel mark.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Revolution Medicines (RVMD) +40% – Shares jumped after reporting positive Phase 3 data for daraxonrasib in metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, with the RASolute 302 trial meeting both primary endpoints of progression-free and overall survival. Patients treated with the oral therapy showed a median overall survival of 13.2 months versus 6.7 months on chemotherapy, marking a significant improvement in a hard-to-treat population, with the company planning to submit the data to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for approval under a priority review pathway.
  • IDEAYA Biosciences (IDYA) +20% – Shares surged after reporting positive topline results from its Phase 2/3 registrational trial (OPTIMUM-02), evaluating darovasertib in combination with crizotinib as a first-line treatment for metastatic uveal melanoma, developed in partnership with Servier. The strong data supports potential regulatory advancement and positions the combination therapy as a promising option in a hard-to-treat cancer setting, boosting investor confidence.
  • Leggett & Platt (LEG) +12% – Shares jumped after agreeing to be acquired by Somnigroup International in an all-stock deal valued at ~$2.5B, with shareholders set to receive 0.1455 SGI shares per LEG share and own ~9% of the combined entity. The transaction, expected to close by year-end 2026, is seen as strategically complementary with projected $50M in annual cost synergies and immediate EPS accretion, while maintaining Leggett as a standalone business unit within Somnigroup.

Biggest stock losers

  • Replimune Group (REPL) -66% - Shares plunged after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration issued a Complete Response Letter declining approval of its lead asset, RP1, for advanced melanoma in combination with nivolumab, marketed by Bristol Myers Squibb as Opdivo. The setback raises significant concerns over the company’s pipeline and regulatory outlook, triggering a sharp selloff following a trading halt.
  • Children’s Place (PLCE) -22% - Shares tumbled after a weak Q4 marked by a sharp earnings and revenue miss, with sales down 19.4% Y/Y to $329M and comparable sales falling 10.7%, driven by weaker e-commerce traffic, reduced Amazon shipments, and soft consumer demand. Profitability deteriorated significantly as gross margin contracted 500 bps to 23.5% due to tariffs, markdowns, and higher inventory reserves, while operating results swung to a loss, underscoring ongoing execution challenges despite improved cash flow and inventory reduction efforts.
  • Goldman Sachs (GS) -4% – Shares slipped after Q4 results showed weaker-than-expected net interest income and higher provisions for credit losses, signaling potential pressure from the lending environment. While overall earnings beat estimates, driven by strong performance in its Global Banking & Markets division, the upside was partially offset by a sequential decline in wealth management revenue, tempering investor sentiment.
  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) -4% – Shares declined after Raymond James downgraded the stock to Outperform from Strong Buy and trimmed its price target to $29 from $30, citing reduced visibility on growth and near-term catalysts. While analysts see upside and view HPE’s focus on AI profitability over market share as margin-accretive, they flagged weaker-than-expected traction in its Cloud and AI segment and ongoing challenges in the networking business, noting the company’s strategy limits exposure to large-scale cloud and AI projects, potentially constraining long-term growth and market share gains.

data.title: Delta Air Lines continues its premium push
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL) is expanding its premium cabin push after seeing strong demand for higher-priced seats and a continued shift toward premium revenue.

The Atlanta-based carrier is rolling out a major cabin upgrade that will bring its next-generation Delta One suite to new Airbus A350-1000 jets and add suites to its A330-200/300 fleet for the first time. The airline says the move is part of a more than $1B investment aimed at keeping its premium product ahead of other U.S. carriers.

The new A350-1000 experience, due in early 2027, will feature sliding privacy doors, a longer lie-flat bed, improved storage, and upgraded comfort touches such as pillow-top cushioning and luxury bedding. Delta (DAL) is also adding a self-serve snack station for Delta One travelers and rolling out larger 4K seatback screens, Bluetooth connectivity, USB-C, and power outlets across both cabins.

Delta (DAL) said the redesign was impacted by a decade of customer and employee feedback, and it expects about 90% of Delta One seats to have privacy doors by 2030. The airline is also extending its new interior design across more than 800 aircraft over the next five years, building on the modern cabin look it introduced in 2025.

Shares of Delta (DAL) were down 1.7% in early Monday morning action.

data.title: Wall Street dips as U.S.-Iran talks fail; Trump looks to blockade Iran's ports
data.description:
Wall Street edged lower Monday after U.S.-Iran talks collapsed, with Washington signaling plans to impose a naval blockade on key Iranian ports.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slipped -0.1%, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) dipped -0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) lost -0.6%. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, eight of the 11 S&P segments were in the red, with materials at the bottom. At the other end of the spectrum, energy has been the strongest performer, as oil (CL1:COM) climbed over 4%. 

U.S. Central Command posted Sunday that it will begin enforcing a maritime blockade on all traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports starting Monday, April 13, at 10 a.m. ET, following a presidential directive. 

“Investors were hoping for an Iran deal at this weekend's negotiations, and instead, there is no deal, so investors are now back to the drawing board trying to reassess the fair value of stocks now that it's clear that there is no end in sight to the conflict in the Middle East. Anytime there is a repricing in markets, we see volatility,” Clark Bellin, president and chief investment officer of Bellwether Wealth, stated.

Furthermore, U.S. Treasury yields were mostly muted. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) slid 1 basis point to 4.30%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) moved down 1 basis point to 3.78%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) was near even at 4.90%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Albemarle (ALB) climbed +5.1%, while shares of Goldman Sachs (GS)  dropped -4%.

data.title: StubHub to refund $10M to settle FTC complaint over hidden fees
data.description:
StubHub (STUB) has agreed to refund $10M to customers and change how it advertises ticket prices to settle a complaint by the Federal Trade Commission ((FTC)) over deceptive or unfair fees.

While the company will refund a portion of affected buyers’ fees, it does not admit any wrongdoing and disagrees with the agency’s view that it engaged in deceptive practices.

Last week, the FTC filed a lawsuit in the federal Southern District of New York for “misrepresenting the price of its live-event tickets and violations of the Commission’s Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees…by not clearly and conspicuously disclosing the total price.” 

The Unfair or Deceptive Fees rule allows for charges such as government charges, shipping fees, and “optional ancillary goods or service” fees to be excluded. However, the FTC identified instances in which StubHub’s (STUB) displayed ticket prices do not include all mandatory fees and charges.

This settlement would require StubHub (STUB) to provide monetary relief to eligible customers who purchased tickets for U.S. live events between May 12 and 14, 2025, and more prominently display the total price of a transaction on its platform.

data.title: Claude usage more than doubles month over month while Gemini steadily climbs: BNP
data.description:
Anthropic's (ANTHRO) Claude daily average user rate more than doubled from February to March, while Google's (GOOG)(GOOGL) Gemini continued to edge higher in usage as well, according to data from BNP Paribas.

Among chatbot websites, Gemini's share of website visits and average monthly daily average users in March increased to 28% and 16.5%, respectively, from 26.2% and 15.1% in February. Claude visits and average monthly DAUs surged in March to 6.6% and 1.8%, respectively, from 3.6% and 0.8% in February.

"While ChatGPT (OPENAI) remains the category leader, it continued to lose both web traffic and mobile app share in March," said BNP analysts, led by Nick Jones, in an investor report. 

Google not only has the second-most-used chatbot in the market, but it is also making gains in the AI compute market as well, following its new agreement with Broadcom (AVGO) to build 3.5GW of capacity for Anthropic.

"This underscores the strength and durability of AI infrastructure demand and reinforces GOOGL's strong positioning, likely adding tens of billions of dollars to backlog," Jones said.

Amazon (AMZN) has also positioned itself nicely in the AI market. Last week, Uber (UBER) announced it was expanding its use of Amazon's Gravitron4 and Trainium3 processors.

"Uber's use of AMZN’s custom chips reinforces AWS' AI value proposition, supporting incremental demand and monetization," Jones added.

This follows Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's recent comments that AWS' AI-related annual recurring revenue has hit $15B and its chips ARR hit $20B.

The release of Meta's (META) Muse Spark AI model prompted a surge in downloads for the Meta AI app.

"We view the model release as an indication that META is making progress on its super intelligence efforts, helping rationalize its heavy spending on AI," Jones noted.

data.title: The AI boom unwinds as tech multiples drop from 40x to 20x, Apollo says
data.description:
Technology stock valuations have retreated sharply, returning to levels seen before the surge driven by artificial intelligence enthusiasm, according to Apollo’s chief economist, Torsten Slok.

The reset reflects a notable compression in forward price-to-earnings ratios across both the S&P 500 (SP500) and its information technology sector, signaling a broader shift in investor sentiment.

Slok noted that valuations, which had expanded to roughly 40 times forward earnings at the height of the AI-driven rally, have now fallen to around 20 times—effectively unwinding much of the premium attached to growth expectations during the boom. 

The repricing comes amid a broader market pullback fueled by escalating geopolitical tensions between the United States and Iran. The collapse of diplomatic talks and Washington’s move toward a naval blockade have driven oil prices sharply higher, with crude (CL1:COM) rising above $100 per barrel and adding pressure to global equities. 

Rising energy costs and heightened uncertainty have weighed on risk appetite, prompting investors to reassess earnings outlooks and valuation multiples. While the decline reduces speculative excess, it underscores a more cautious market environment as geopolitical risks and macroeconomic headwinds increasingly shape investor positioning. 

S&P 500 Funds: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX). 

Info Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT).

data.title: TotalEnergies makes discovery offshore Congo; plans to explore Black Sea with Turkey's TPAO
data.description:
TotalEnergies (TTE) said Monday it made a significant discovery offshore Congo as part ‌of a drilling campaign to expand its Moho field, following the drilling of the MHNM-6 NFW exploration well targeting the Moho G structure.

The Moho G well, which ​encountered a column of 160 meters of oil ​and gas, combined with the previous discovery at the nearby Moho F structure, represent recoverable resources estimated at ~100M barrels, which the company said it plans to develop as a tie-back to the existing Moho facilities.

TotalEnergies' (TTE) current production at ​the Moho deep offshore field consists of ‌two ⁠floating units with combined output of ~90K boe/day.

Separately, TotalEnergies (TTE) said it signed ‌a preliminary agreement with state-owned ​Turkish company ​TPAO to explore ⁠Turkey's Black ​Sea waters for ​hydrocarbons.

The MoU provides a framework for technical collaboration, including a joint assessment of exploration opportunities in the Black Sea region and internationally.

data.title: Revolution Medicines surges on data for pancreatic cancer asset daraxonrasib
data.description:
• Revolution Medicines (RVMD) is up ~36% in premarket trading Monday after releasing positive phase 3 data on its metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma candidate daraxonrasib.
  • The RASolute 302 trial met both primary endpoints, progression-free and overall survival.
  • Patients enrolled in the study had failed on other therapies. Results showed that those receiving daraxonrasib oral 300 mg daily had a median overall survival of 13.2 months compared to 6.7 months for those on chemotherapy. 
  • The company said it would submit the data to the US FDA as part of an NDA under a Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher.

data.title: Stock market slips as U.S.-Iran talks fail; Trump looks to blockade Iran's ports
data.description:
Wall Street fell on Monday after U.S.-Iran talks collapsed and Washington announced it will begin a blockade of Iranian ports.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.2%, the Dow (DJI) -0.6%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.3%. 

U.S. Central Command posted Sunday that it will begin enforcing a maritime blockade on all traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports starting Monday, April 13, at 10 a.m. ET, following a presidential directive.

Treasury yields were mixed across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) edged lower to 4.33%, down 0.8 basis points. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose to 3.82%, up 1.4 basis points, while the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) ticked higher to 4.92%, up 0.7 basis points.

On the economic calendar, investors will watch the existing home sales report during market hours.
